name: LIEF (Library to Instrument Executable Formats)

auth.notes: Local binary analysis library; no auth

performance.notes: C++ backend with Python bindings; fast parsing even for large binaries

agent_readiness.notes: Cross-format executable binary parsing and modification. import lief. binary = lief.parse('sample.exe'). binary.exports.functions. binary.imports. binary.sections. Supports PE, ELF, Mach-O, OAT (Android), DEX (Dalvik), VDEX. Binary modification: binary.add_section(new_section). binary.write('modified.exe'). Inject code sections. Patch GOT entries. Android app analysis. C++ core with Python API. More comprehensive than pefile (PE-only). Used in security research and tooling.

gotchas: C++ dependency — binary wheel available on PyPI for common platforms
Older docs may refer to outdated API (API changed significantly in v0.13+)
Android OAT/DEX support is less mature than PE/ELF
Binary patching requires understanding of format internals
best_when: Cross-format binary analysis (PE, ELF, Mach-O, Android)
When pefile's PE-only scope is insufficient
Android APK/DEX binary analysis and modification
avoid_when: PE-only analysis (pefile simpler for Windows malware)
Dynamic analysis (use Frida or dynamic tracing tools)
summary: LIEF parses and modifies PE, ELF, Mach-O, and Android binary formats. lief.parse('binary') works across formats. Imports, exports, sections, binary patching (add_section, write). Android OAT/DEX support. C++ core with Python API. More comprehensive than pefile for cross-platform binary analysis. Used for security research, binary instrumentation, and Android APK analysis.
